subtopic: Index Models and the Arbitrage Pricing Theory
title: Index Models and the Arbitrage Pricing Theory: Which of the following factors is considered when constructing a...
summary: Identify factors in multifactor models for analyzing asset returns.
prompt: Which of the following factors is considered when constructing a multifactor model?

options.text: When constructing a multifactor model, various factors are considered,

solution: When constructing a multifactor model, various factors are considered, including market risk, firm-specific risk, and macroeconomic indicators. Multifactor models aim to capture a more comprehensive view of the risks affecting asset returns, allowing for a better understanding of the relationships between risk factors and expected returns.
